name: ExtensionValueSet_Compose_Property
title: R5: Property to return if client doesn't override (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ValueSet.compose.property` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ValueSet.compose.property` 0..* `string`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ValueSet.compose.property` 0..* `string`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ValueSet.compose.property` has a context of ValueSet.compose based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ValueSet`.
Element `ValueSet.compose.property` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Property to return if client doesn't override (new)
element.comment:
Element `ValueSet.compose.property` has a context of ValueSet.compose based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ValueSet`.
Element `ValueSet.compose.property` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Note that property names can clash, so using a URI is recommended.

element.short: Property to return if client doesn't override
element.definition: A property to return in the expansion, if the client doesn't ask for any particular properties. May be either a code from the code system definition (convenient) or a the formal URI that refers to the property. The special value '*' means all properties known to the server.
element.comment: Note that property names can clash, so using a URI is recommended.
